title: [cross-impl] Trickle timer half/offset calculation differs across Rust vs Python/Zephyr
description: Rust/lichen-rpl/src/trickle.rs:113 uses `half = interval / 2`; `offset = rand_offset % half` (range ≈ I/2). Python (trickle.py:69) and Zephyr (lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/trickle.c:41, updated in a31840159) use `half = (interval + 1)/2`; range = interval - half. Leads to different transmit_time distributions for odd intervals (e.g. I=5: Rust [now+2,now+4), others [now+3,now+5)). Violates "same algorithm different behavior" rule. Reset condition also diverges (state vs transmit_time==0). See RFC 6206 §4.2, test vectors needed for parity. Matches project-LICHEN-verh / Worker23 bias fix but Rust not updated. Port comment in trickle.c is now inaccurate.
close_reason: Aligned Rust trickle half/range calculation and updated port comment in C to eliminate cross-impl divergence for odd intervals per RFC 6206 §4.2. Commit d9d8789. Tests pass for existing cases; new vectors follow-on if needed.
